purpose: Requirements for generating a reusable Area 01 terrain art kit from current source maps. This is planning data only and does not wire art or collision into runtime.

pipeline_axes.runtime_object_model: platform_objects plus interactive_scene_objects plus foreground_occluders plus scene_hooks
pipeline_axes.art_style: pixel_inspired underwater reef/cave art with readable silhouettes and layered depth

stage_context.topology_rule: Open surface water over a continuous seafloor shelf with cave mouths leading into authored route pockets.
stage_context.collision_rule: Collision must be generated from source-map polygons, never from generated image pixels.

asset_families.generation_strategy: wide scenery layer or repeatable strip
asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D or ParallaxLayer background
acceptance: Shows surface/waterline without implying a ceiling blocker.
Contains no pickups, gates, hazards, route icons, or foreground terrain.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D or ParallaxLayer background
acceptance: Creates depth behind foreground terrain.
Cannot be mistaken for a foreground collision boundary at camera scale.

asset_families.generation_strategy: wide transparent overlay or shader-friendly texture
asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D or ColorRect/TextureRect overlay
acceptance: Adds underwater light shafts and depth haze.
Does not obscure terrain lips, cave mouths, pickups, or scan targets.

asset_families.generation_strategy: seamless fill texture or atlas fill region
asset_families.runtime_role: Texture applied to Polygon2D terrain fill or repeated Sprite2D fill
acceptance: Reads as organic reef/cave mass, not flat gray shape.
Tiles without obvious square stamping at 1280x720 camera scale.
Does not define collision independently.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D strip following top playable edges
acceptance: Clearly marks the open-water side of the continuous seafloor.
Left/middle/right pieces fit seamlessly.
Can follow long shelves without looking like repeated rectangular stamps.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D strip following underside/overhang edges
acceptance: Makes overhangs and ceilings read as solid.
Does not visually close navigable cave mouths.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D strip following vertical wall edges
acceptance: Makes descent shafts and side walls readable.
Can be mirrored left/right without obvious lighting errors.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D strip or segmented trim along sloped polygon edges
acceptance: Supports irregular source-map polygons without forcing grid-square terrain.
Keeps sloped playable boundaries visually honest.

source_map_drivers: all solid_terrain entries with concave or protruding corners
asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D cap pieces at trim junctions
acceptance: Hides seams between top lips, vertical walls, and underside strips.
Does not introduce fake protrusions beyond source collision.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D narrow ledge top/underside
acceptance: Reads as small interior shelf, not as cargo or scan target.
Does not make narrow lanes look blocked.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D trim group aligned to cave mouth polygon
acceptance: Reads as the first safe dive hole.
Clearly leaves the navigable entrance open.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D trim group aligned to cave mouth polygon
acceptance: Reads as a wider banking route entrance.
Supports shell/coral identity without hiding resource pockets.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D trim group plus warm vent color cues
acceptance: Communicates a deeper hazard/reward pocket.
Does not look like a locked gate.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D trim group with blue crystal/deep-route cues
acceptance: Reads as deeper vertical route.
Works with return-current visual without becoming clutter.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D trim group with wreck/pressure cues
acceptance: Reads as a branch toward a wreck and pressure gate.
Does not make the whole branch look unreachable unless the gate hook owns that state.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D quiet locked-route frame
acceptance: Reads as future-locked and quieter than current objectives.
Does not look like cargo, a pickup, or an active scan reward.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D decorative prop placements
acceptance: Creates safe shallow route identity.
Never hides cave-mouth openings or current-run pickups.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D decorative prop placements
acceptance: Creates Shell Reef identity without turning shells into false pickups.
Stays visually lower priority than cargo resources.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D vent prop plus optional visual-only bubbles/glow
acceptance: Reads as environmental hazard/reward area.
Hazard state remains owned by Area2D, not sprite pixels.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D decorative and material-read props
acceptance: Creates deep-route visual identity.
Does not replace actual pickup resource sprites.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D wreck silhouettes/debris
acceptance: Reads as wreck promise and route landmark.
Does not create fake blockers unless source-map collision says so.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D gate visual aligned to gate Area2D
acceptance: Reads as lock/constraint, not cargo.
Its blocking state is controlled by explicit gate data.

asset_families.generation_strategy: one_by_one or short animated strip later
asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D or GPUParticles2D visual for current path
acceptance: Reads as movement/flow support, not a resource or gate.
Does not obscure the cave path.

asset_families.runtime_role: Sprite2D visuals paired with separate pickup/scan Area2D hooks
acceptance: Cargo resources read differently from scan targets.
Future promises are visually quieter than current-run resources.
Sprites stay small enough to preserve exploration scale.

validation_rules: Every solid terrain polygon can be skinned with fill plus readable rim/lip assets.
Every cave mouth frame leaves the navigable center visually open.
No generated preview or background image becomes runtime collision authority.
Collision remains generated from source-map polygons.
Parallax layers contain no runtime-controlled objects.
Square prop packs are used only for compact decorative/resource props.
Terrain strips, cave frames, gates, and wreck chunks are one-by-one, strip, or custom-wide assets.
At 1280x720 camera scale, foreground solid terrain is distinguishable from background parallax.
Current-run resources, scans, future locks, and decorative props have distinct visual reads.
next_pipeline_step: Use generate2dsprite to create the first terrain kit assets from these requirements, then compose a preview over docs/planning/maps/area_01_surface_floor_geometry_v1.json before runtime wiring.
